# ugrant agent quickstart

Use this file when an AI agent needs to start using `ugrant`.

Full guide: https://www.ugrant.sh/llms-full.txt
Repo for coding agents: https://github.com/anulman/ugrant

## What ugrant is

`ugrant` is a local OAuth 2.0 token broker and exec wrapper for scripts, shell tools, CLIs, and local daemons.

It keeps grant material local, refreshes access tokens on use, and injects only child-safe environment variables into the downstream process.

## Intended agent flow

1. Install `ugrant`
2. Run `ugrant init`
3. Run `ugrant profile add ...`
4. Run `ugrant login --profile ...`
5. Pause for human OAuth consent if needed
6. Resume and run the target command with `ugrant exec --profile ... -- <command>`

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSLo /tmp/ugrant-install.sh https://www.ugrant.sh/install.sh && sh /tmp/ugrant-install.sh
```

The installer prefers minisign verification when `minisign` is available, and falls back to checksum verification for compatibility.

## Consent boundary

Do not assume OAuth consent is fully autonomous.

Agents can handle setup and command wiring, but the human may still need to complete approval in the browser during `ugrant login`.

## Core commands

Initialize state:

```bash
ugrant init
```

Add a Google IMAP profile:

```bash
ugrant profile add \
  --name gmail \
  --service google-imap \
  --client-id <id> \
  --client-secret <secret>
```

Log in:

```bash
ugrant login --profile gmail
```

Run a downstream tool with injected credentials:

```bash
ugrant exec --profile gmail -- python sync_mail.py
```

Inspect status:

```bash
ugrant status --profile gmail
```

Run diagnostics:

```bash
ugrant doctor
```
